title: Asteroid 2022 WJ1 over Lake Erie
narrative: Asteroid 2022 WJ1 was discovered only hours before it entered the atmosphere over Lake Erie on 19 November 2022 — one of a handful of asteroids ever detected before impact. As its fragments fell they drifted on the wind and were swept by the KBUF (Buffalo) NEXRAD weather radar. With no human in the loop, MetDetect automatically flagged the elongated “debris streak” shown here — the falling meteorites — at 1.00 confidence.
